evidence_summary: bs being previously removed from the portions so dis- tributed, and put aside: it was only the back-bone to- gether with the ribs and attached meat that were thrown into the fire and so destroyed. The sentiment which prompted this eating of the deceased was a double one: the survivors knew where the dead actually were and so could not be frightened by their spirits, while the disposal of the corpse in this manner pre- vented its going bad and stinking. The liver was eaten, but the entrails, heart and lungs buried, the spot being marked by three sticks about a foot high, éachi wound round with grass-rope, and stuck closely’ apposed.
